phdOffers.title: Industry PhD Scholarship – Digital Finance CRC (Applied Finance & Compliance)
phdOffers.subject: Industry-linked PhD researching digital finance innovations: tokenisation, automated market makers, pricing models, and the legal/compliance landscape of smart contracts and regulated digital markets.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: AUD $70,000 p.a. tax-free stipend for 3.5 years; tuition fee offset; $10,000 project training package; industry placement.
phdOffers.notes: Re-verified 2026-07-12: DFCRC page still shows 'Applications now open'. 6 partner universities unchanged (Curtin, Macquarie, UNSW, UTS, UWA, UOW). DFCRC homepage now states 'funded for 3 years'; Macquarie/UTS partner pages still state 3.5 years — treated as authoritative per-university detail.

phdOffers.title: Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) Banking & Finance – Funded Doctoral Program
phdOffers.subject: Full doctoral program in banking and finance including corporate finance, asset pricing, empirical corporate governance, and financial economics research training.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: Vast majority of admitted students receive tuition waiver and stipend (~AUD $38–48K p.a. per UNSW Business FAQ); MPDBS pathway year also fully funded.
phdOffers.notes: Re-verified 2026-07-12 by direct fetch: EOI window confirmed May 1–July 31 each year for following-year February intake; still open (11 days remaining as of as-of date).

phdOffers.title: Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) Accounting, Auditing & Taxation – Funded Program
phdOffers.subject: Doctoral research in financial accounting, auditing, management accounting, and taxation with integrated research methods training.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: Automatic scholarship consideration for most admitted HDR students; tuition waiver and ~AUD $38–48K stipend typical.
phdOffers.notes: Re-verified 2026-07-12: shares same EOI window (May 1–July 31) as Banking & Finance PhD, confirmed via UNSW Business School page.

phdOffers.title: Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) Risk & Actuarial Studies – Funded Program
phdOffers.subject: PhD in actuarial studies, risk analytics, superannuation, regulatory capital, and investment/asset-liability modelling.

phdOffers.title: CSIRO Industry PhD Program – Risk-Aware and Trustworthy AI Agents for Adaptive Quantitative Trading
phdOffers.subject: Industry PhD co-supervised by RMIT, CSIRO, and fintech partner WX Wealthverse: multi-agent reinforcement-learning framework for adaptive quantitative trading with real-time risk governance, explainable AI, and audit/compliance-suitable trade rationales.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: CSIRO Industry PhD 4-year scholarship package, approx. AUD $48,000+ p.a. tax-exempt (2026 rate) plus a AUD $13,000+ p.a. Project Expense and Development package.

Hosted at RMIT (School of Accounting, Information Systems and Supply Chain); CSIRO Technology research unit; industry partner WX Wealthverse Pty Ltd. Application close date: open until position filled. Apply via RMIT / contact university supervisor Chao Chen.
